stories.title: As Pakistan seeks to broker U.S.–Iran peace, citizens watch for gains at home
stories.description: As Pakistan works to broker peace between the U.S. and Iran, many Pakistanis are watching closely to see whether the diplomatic boost will translate into improvements at home.

stories.title: What would happen next if Iran agreed to surrender its highly enriched uranium
stories.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Scott Roecker of the Nuclear Threat Initiative about how the U.S. might go about moving highly enriched uranium out of Iran.

stories.title: Trump extends U.S. ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan's request
stories.description: The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.

stories.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire nears its end. And, Fed chair nominee faces tough hearing
stories.description: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing today.

stories.title: Ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon
stories.description: The ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon. President Trump says a U.S. delegation is going to Pakistan for talks, but Iran hasn't confirmed their attendance.

stories.title: Gulf aluminum shipments stuck as Strait of Hormuz blockades put a strain on supply
stories.description: A U.S. blockade and conflict in the Strait of Hormuz have stranded Gulf aluminum shipments and driven prices to a four-year high, exposing global supply vulnerabilities and deepening trade strain.

stories.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
stories.description: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

stories.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.description: Two soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention, Israel's military says.

stories.title: Trump says US will not lift Hormuz blockade until deal made with Iran
stories.description: The US president's comments come amid uncertainty over whether Iran will attend peace talks in Pakistan this week.

stories.title: US intercepts and seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship, Trump says
stories.description: Tehran vows retaliation soon for what it calls an "act of armed piracy", which comes as the US prepares for a second round of talks.

stories.title: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
stories.description: Lyse Doucet speaks to Ebrahim Azizi, who says Iran "will decide the right of passage" through the crucial shipping route.

stories.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
stories.description: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

stories.title: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
stories.description: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?

stories.title: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
stories.description: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.title: UK and France to lead defensive mission in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Sir Keir Starmer has said further details of the plan will come after a meeting in London next week.

stories.title: Trump extends ceasefire, says giving Iran time to negotiate
stories.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday extended the US ceasefire with Iran to allow more time for talks, just hours before hostilities were set to resume, but he vowed to maintain a blockade of Tehran's ports. Trump indefinitely pushed back the end of the truce, crediting a request fr...

stories.title: Ukraine reopens damaged Druzhba pipeline to unlock €90 billion EU loan
stories.description: Kyiv said Tuesday it has repaired the damaged Druzhba oil pipeline, clearing the last obstacle to a long-delayed €90 billion EU loan. The move forces Ukraine into the uncomfortable position of resuming Russian oil flows to Hungary and Slovakia even as Ukraine President Volodymyr ...

stories.title: France freezes some spending to offset €6 billion fallout from Iran war
stories.description: France says the economic fallout from the Iran war has cost France at least 6 billion euros, as the government has rolled out support measures for businesses and households, increased defence spending and faced surging borrowing costs. To offset this while meeting its budget defi...

stories.title: Was Donald Trump 'blocked' from using the nuclear codes against Iran?
stories.description: Former CIA analyst Larry Johnson alleged on a podcast this week that President Donald Trump wanted to use the nuclear codes against Iran during an emergency White House meeting, but that he was stopped by General Dan Caine, Chairman of his Joint Chiefs of Staff. He described the ...

stories.title: EU resumes aid to Ethiopia suspended over Tigray war
stories.description: In tonight's edition, the European Union is set to resume budgetary aid to Ethiopia, which was suspended when a brutal civil war broke out in 2020. Also, Nigeria’s jet fuel shortage is being driven by disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz that have curtailed global supplies. And fi...

stories.title: US-Iran talks are in doubt as ceasefire nears an end
stories.description: US officials have announced that Vice President JD Vance's trip to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran is on hold, as a two-week truce was set to expire and both countries warned that, without a deal, they were prepared to resume fighting. Iran said on Tuesday it had still yet to ...

stories.title: Macron to host Lebanon's PM Nawaf Salam
stories.description: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam is set to meet with French President Emmanuel Macron at the Élysée Palace late on Tuesday, with the president expected to reaffirm his support for the ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, three days after the death of a French peacekeeper in...

stories.title: Top Lebanese official makes warning
stories.description: Israel carried out an airstrike in the Wadi Houjeir valley in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, according to local outlet L’Orient-Le Jour, despite a truce that came into effect on Friday. The Israeli army has also accused Hezbollah of violating the ceasefire, while the Iran-backed gr...

stories.title: US issues more Iran sanctions on eve of possible talks in Pakistan
stories.description: The Trump administration targets 14 individuals and entities over alleged links to Iran's arms industry.

stories.title: EU eyes options as Iran conflict threatens jet fuel shortages
stories.description: The blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is causing concern in Europe, which relies heavily on imported jet fuel.

stories.title: Trump announces Iran ceasefire extension but says blockade remains
stories.description: US president says attacks will be held off until Iranian leaders 'come up with a unified proposal' to end war.

stories.title: Video: Israeli soldiers sing national anthem on ruins of Lebanese town
stories.description: Israeli soldiers were seen singing the Israeli national anthem on the ruins of a demolished town in southern Lebanon.

stories.title: Nicaragua’s president says Trump ‘mentally deranged’ over war on Iran
stories.description: Nicaraguan President has criticised his US counterpart Donald Trump, over the war on Iran.

stories.title: US forces detain Iran-linked tanker Tifani with ceasefire talks on edge
stories.description: Ship, detained under US policy to stop all Tehran-linked vessels, is under sanctions for smuggling Iranian crude.

stories.title: ‘At the request of CDF Munir, PM Shehbaz’, Trump announces extension in ceasefire until Iran submits proposal
stories.description: Key developments: Trump has announced an extension in the ceasefire with Iran until Tehran submits a “unified proposal”; however, a naval blockade of Iranian ports will remain in place; JD Vance’s visit to Pakistan ’“not happening today”, says White House official PM Shehbaz than...

stories.title: Downing Street denies exerting pressure on civil servants to approve Mandelson’s appointment as envoy to US
stories.description: Downing Street on Tuesday denied a claim from a former official that it had applied pressure on civil servants to approve the appointment of Peter Mandelson as UK envoy to Washington and seemed to dismiss security concerns. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer remained mired in a scand...

stories.title: Israeli army jails soldiers for vandalising Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.description: The Israeli military on Tuesday said two soldiers would receive 30 days of military detention and be removed from combat duty over the destruction of a statue of Jesus in southern Lebanon. The decision comes after widespread condemnation of a photo shared online showing an Israel...

stories.title: ‘Reprehensible’: Pakistan condemns storming of Al Aqsa Mosque compound, raising of Israeli flag in its courtyard
stories.description: Pakistan condemned in “strongest terms” on Tuesday the storming of the Al Aqsa Mosque compound and the raising of Israel’s flag in its courtyard, terming the acts “reprehensible”. The condemnation issued by the Foreign Office said “these reprehensible acts constitute a blatant vi...

stories.title: Diplomatic flurry as US-Iran truce deadline nears
stories.description: • US team heading to Islamabad today; Iran also ‘considering’ sending negotiators• Trump extends ceasefire by a day, says further extension ‘highly unlikely’• Pezeshkian criticises ‘bitter, contradictory’ messages from US; Iran speaker says engaged in talks but also ready for ‘ne...

stories.title: ‘Biggest-ever’ US-Philippines war games begin
stories.description: MANILA: Thousands of American and Philippine troops, joined for the first time by a significant contingent of Japanese forces, began annual military exercises on Monday set against the backdrop of the Middle East war. The war games will feature live-fire exercises in the north of...

stories.title: Trump extends ceasefire until Iranian proposal is submitted, talks are over
stories.description: A senior commander in Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps threatened to destroy the region’s oil industry if war with the U.S. resumes

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Trump extends ceasefire until Iran submits proposal
stories.description: An Iranian commander had threatened to destroy the region's oil industry if war resumed with the U.S.

stories.title: Iran says U.S. violated truce; non-committal on Pakistan talks
stories.description: As of 7:30 p.m. Pakistan time, a formal response about Iran’s participation “is still awaited”, Pakistan’s Information Minister said in a statement

stories.title: Iran to restart domestic flights; charts phased airspace reopening
stories.description: Iranian government sources told The Hindu that they plan to first reallow overflight operations that will bring a huge respite to all international airlines connecting Europe with Asia, including Indian carriers

stories.title: Trump says U.S. in ‘very, very strong’ position for Iran talks
stories.description: The U.S. President also told Iran it could boost the chances of success in peace talks with the United States by freeing eight women that he said face execution

stories.title: Can Israel’s economy sustain conflicts on multiple fronts?
stories.description: The Bank of Israel’s preliminary estimates suggest that Israel’s war with Iran came at the cost of 35 billion shekels ($11.52 billion) in budgetary expenses. Its operations in Gaza till 2025 cost the country around 8.6% of its annual gross domestic product.

stories.title: Iran’s participation in talks with US remains under consideration — Iranian diplomat
stories.description: Ambassador to Tunisia Mir Masoud Hosseinian says Iran’s participation in the negotiations depends on the actions of the American side

stories.title: Iran will not recognize US-announced ceasefire extension — TV
stories.description: Tehran will act in accordance with its own interests, the statement says

stories.title: Trump says extended ceasefire with Iran until its ‘unified proposal’
stories.description: The US president says the naval blockade of the country's ports will also be extended for this period

stories.title: Vance’s trip to Islamabad for talks with Iran put on hold indefinitely — Axios
stories.description: Sources cited by the news outlet say that the vice president is taking part in additional political meetings at the White House
